expectations.text: Identifies issues found by multiple personas (cross-persona patterns)
expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude has no concept of 'cross-persona' aggregation because it never ran 8 distinct persona agents. It might summarize test findings generically but would not produce a structured list of issues grouped by how many personas encountered them.

expectations.text: Highlights persona-specific findings not shared by other personas
expectations.evidence: Persona-specific unique findings assume 8 persona runs to compare against. Without the skill's parallel execution, there is no multi-persona result set to diff. Naive Claude would produce a flat list of issues, not a segmented per-persona breakdown.

expectations.text: Ranks findings by severity and cross-persona agreement count
expectations.evidence: The severity×breadth ranking formula (SKILL.md Step 3 Top 5 section) is skill-specific. Without the persona agreement counts (since no multi-persona run occurred), naive Claude cannot compute this composite score. It might rank by severity alone.

expectations.text: Produces structured synthesis-report.md using skill template
expectations.evidence: synthesis-report-template.md is a Conduction-specific artifact in the test-counsel skill directory. Without the skill, naive Claude would not know this template exists and would write an unstructured markdown summary instead.

notes: All synthesis behaviors depend on having run the multi-persona parallel test step first. Without the skill's Step 2, there is no multi-persona result set, making Steps 3 and 4 structurally impossible to perform correctly.
